title: Step 1: Remove the private endpoint from .

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To view private endpoints, you must have [Project Read Only](<Project Read Only>)
access to the project.

To delete private endpoints, you must have [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access
or higher to the project.

Users with [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) access must add themselves to the
project as a `Project Owner`.

sections.heading: Considerations
sections.body:
For multi-region clusters, you must create a
private endpoint for each region with a node.

If you're performing maintenance on a multi-region cluster, do not
alter or remove private endpoints until maintenance is complete to
avoid cluster downtime.

If you're moving from a multi-region to a single-region cluster,
you can remove old private endpoints only after verifying that your cluster
is fully functional in the new single-region setup and you've directed all traffic
through the new single-region private endpoint.

For a full list of considerations, see [Considerations](private-endpoint-considerations).

sections.heading: Connect from a Private Endpoint
sections.body:
> **Important:** For considerations about private endpoint-aware connection
> strings, see
> [Private Endpoint-Aware Connection Strings](private-endpoint-connection-strings).

Use a private endpoint-aware connection string to connect to an
Atlas cluster with the following procedure:

Click Connect for the cluster to
which you want to connect.

> **Important:** **Skip this step** if Atlas indicates in the
> Setup connection security step that you have at least
> one database user configured in your project. To manage existing
> database users, see [Configure Database Users](mongodb-users).

To access the cluster, you need a MongoDB user with
access to the desired database or databases on the
cluster in your project. If your
project has no MongoDB users, Atlas prompts you to create a new
user with the [built-in role](manual/reference/built-in-roles) role.

- Enter the new user's Username.

- Enter a Password for this new user or click
  Autogenerate Secure Password.

- Click Create Database User to save the user.

Use this user to connect to your cluster in the
following step.

Once you have added a database user, click
Choose Your Connection Method.

sections.heading: Endpoints
sections.body:
To return the details of the AWS (Amazon Web Services) private endpoint you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints aws describe <privateEndpointId> [options]

```

To list all AWS (Amazon Web Services) private endpoints in a project using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints aws list [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas privateEndpoints aws describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-aws-describe) and [atlas privateEndpoints aws list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-aws-list).

sections.heading: Interface Endpoints
sections.body:
To return the AWS (Amazon Web Services) private endpoint interface that you specify. using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints aws interfaces describe <interfaceEndpointId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas privateEndpoints aws interfaces describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-aws-interfaces-describe).

Atlas UI

Click Dedicated Cluster for a private endpoint
for your dedicated Atlas cluster. (default)

Azure Private Link

Atlas CLI

sections.heading: Endpoints
sections.body:
To return the details of the Azure (Microsoft Azure) private endpoint you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints azure describe <privateEndpointId> [options]

```

To list all Azure (Microsoft Azure) private endpoints in a project using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints azure list [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas privateEndpoints azure describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-azure-describe) and [atlas privateEndpoints azure list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-azure-list).

sections.heading: Interface Endpoints
sections.body:
To return the Azure (Microsoft Azure) private endpoint interface that you specify. using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints azure interfaces describe <privateEndpointResourceId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas privateEndpoints azure interfaces describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-azure-interfaces-describe).

Atlas UI

Click Dedicated Cluster for a private endpoint
for your dedicated Atlas cluster. (default)

GCP Private Service Connect

Atlas CLI

sections.heading: Endpoints
sections.body:
To return the details of the Google Cloud private endpoint you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints gcp describe <privateEndpointId> [options]

```

To list all Google Cloud private endpoints in a project using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints gcp list [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas privateEndpoints gcp describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-gcp-describe) and [atlas privateEndpoints gcp list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-gcp-list).

sections.heading: Interface Endpoints
sections.body:
To return the Google Cloud private endpoint interface that you specify. using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints gcp interfaces describe <id> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas privateEndpoints gcp interfaces describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-gcp-interfaces-describe).

Atlas UI

Click Dedicated Cluster for a private endpoint
for your dedicated Atlas cluster. (default)

sections.heading: Remove a Private Endpoint from Atlas
Endpoints
sections.body:
To delete the AWS (Amazon Web Services) private endpoint you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints aws delete <privateEndpointId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas privateEndpoints aws delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-aws-delete).

sections.heading: Interface Endpoints
sections.body:
To delete the AWS (Amazon Web Services) private endpoint interface you specify using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas privateEndpoints aws interfaces delete <interfaceEndpointId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas privateEndpoints aws interfaces delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-privateEndpoints-aws-interfaces-delete).

Atlas UI

- Click the Private Endpoint tab.

- Next to the private endpoint you want to remove, click
  Terminate.

- To confirm, click Confirm in the dialog box.

Azure Private Link

Atlas CLI
